title: hemizonia congesta subsp. leucocephala
intro: Hemizonia congesta subsp. leucocephala, also known as Mountain Madroña or San Luis Valley Madroña, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the southwestern United States. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots and leaves, which have been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Hemizonia congesta subsp. leucocephala are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for digestive issues, wounds, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and salves, often made by drying the roots and leaves and then infusing them in hot water or oil.
